task_completion.description: Verify the bcWebCam application is open.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main window of the bcWebCam application is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the Options dialog is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Options dialog window for bcWebCam is open and visible on the screen, typically accessed via the gear icon in the bottom toolbar.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'Delete recognized Barcode after' checkbox is checked.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Inside the Options dialog, there is a visible checkmark in the checkbox labeled 'Delete recognized Barcode after' (or similar wording).

task_completion.description: Verify the timeout value is set to 500 seconds.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text box or number field associated with the 'Delete recognized Barcode after' setting contains the exact value '500'.

task_completion.description: Verify the OK button is clicked to apply the settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OK button in the Options dialog is seen being clicked (highlighted/pressed), or the Options dialog is successfully closed in subsequent screenshots after the correct settings were applied.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface to configure the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the bcWebCam GUI (mouse clicks, typing in the dialog) rather than terminal commands or text editors modifying configuration files.

integrity.description: Verify the settings were changed in the actual bcWebCam application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The window title, icons, and UI elements correspond to the genuine bcWebCam software.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use scripts or command-line tools to bypass the UI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows (like PowerShell or Command Prompt) or script editors are visible executing commands to change bcWebCam settings.
